authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Funeral Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal regulator responsible for the Funeral Rule, and this official business guidance explains funeral-provider price-list, itemized statement, embalming, telephone price, preneed, and recordkeeping workflows used as evidence inputs for funeral service analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: request framing, price-disclosure evidence review, and exception screening
used_for: General Price List, Casket Price List, outer burial container, embalming, preneed, and Statement of Funeral Goods and Services Selected terminology
price-disclosure completion metrics
boundary against price, refund, and disclosure overreach

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 - Funeral Industry Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR text is the primary federal Funeral Rule regulation governing disclosure, statement, and recordkeeping controls. It is authoritative for determining which evidence cannot be skipped when reporting price-disclosure or arrangement-completion metrics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control baseline and recordkeeping evidence validation
used_for: minimum evidence expectations for disclosure metrics
recordkeeping and statement controls
refusal triggers for signed-contract-only or unsupported completion claims

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Directors' Handbook on Death Registration and Fetal Death Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Center for Health Statistics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC/NCHS publishes the national vital-statistics workflow manual for funeral directors. It is authoritative for death-registration, demographic intake, medical certification, registrar, and filing dependencies that govern readiness and cycle-time analytics.
used_for: EDRS and death certificate status semantics
medical certification and registrar dependency caveats
readiness metric blockers when vital-record state is missing or conflicting

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Guide to Funeral and Cemetery Purchases
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs Cemetery and Funeral Bureau
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's funeral and cemetery regulator publishes official public guidance on right to control disposition, funeral price lists, itemized statements, embalming authorization, cremation, hydrolysis, cemetery purchases, and licensing boundaries. It is used as a concrete state regulator operating reference, not as universal state law.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: jurisdiction-sensitive funeral, cemetery, and consumer-service baseline review
used_for: state regulator terminology for funeral and cemetery artifacts
jurisdiction caveats and authority boundaries
consumer complaint and policy-exception escalation posture

authoritative_sources.title: Conference Model Disposition Standards
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Conference of Funeral Service Examining Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Conference is a recognized funeral service examining-board body. Its model disposition standards provide regulator and practitioner workflow terms for authorizing agent, authorization, identification, custody, burial, cremation, and disposition records used to define analytics prerequisites and failure modes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: disposition authorization, identity, custody, and release evidence review
used_for: authorizing agent and disposition authorization semantics
identity and custody failure modes
release-of-remains escalation triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Model Cremation Law and Explanation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CANA is a recognized cremation professional association. Its model law names cremation authorization, identification, authorizing agent, medical-device disclosure, disposition of cremated remains, and crematory record concepts that constrain cremation-related metrics and caveats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cremation authorization and crematory record evidence review
used_for: cremation authorization completeness metrics
hazardous implant and identification caveats
release-of-cremated-remains failure modes

authoritative_sources.title: Application of provisions of the Bloodborne Pathogens standard to Funeral and Nursing Homes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's official interpretation explains bloodborne-pathogen controls for funeral homes and human remains. It is authoritative for identifying when analytics touches safety-sensitive body-handling records that require refusal or routing to licensed and safety owners.
used_for: refusal to provide body-handling or preparation instructions
safety caveats for embalming, transfer, or exposure-sensitive metrics
routing to licensed and safety-controlled owners

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records-management authority. Its public ERM requirements define metadata, retention, retrieval, integrity, access, and audit-trail controls that are relevant to evidence traceability and source-of-truth handling in analytics packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record lineage, metadata, retrieval, and auditability review
used_for: source-lineage requirements
record retention and audit caveats
system-of-record and retrieval dependency expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is an authoritative public baseline for defining, contextualizing, caveating, and updating operational metrics when stakeholders ask for executive, board, investor-style, or external disclosure-ready claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric-definition, caveat, stronger-claim, and disclosure-style review
used_for: metric definition and denominator transparency
trend, benchmark, forecast, and causal-claim caveats
refusal of unsupported board-ready or external disclosure language

workflow_stages.name: request framing and tenant grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm request intent, audience, jurisdiction, facility, reporting period, disposition scope, source hierarchy, and publication context before defining any metric.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: funeral metric definition and denominator selection
workflow_stages.description: Define the metric, denominator, inclusions, exclusions, source system, refresh window, and action threshold for funeral service cycle time, arrangement readiness, Funeral Rule evidence, death-registration, disposition, cremation, cemetery, complaint, and aftercare measures.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: record collection and cross-system reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile funeral home case management, EDRS, document repository, crematory tracking, cemetery management, CRM, complaint, accounting, and reporting warehouse records before asserting metric outcomes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: service resolution and stakeholder communication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare the resolution note, analytical brief, stakeholder communication summary, or blocked-prerequisites response with safe caveats and next-owner context.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: unsupported claim and policy-exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop descriptive analytics from turning into unsupported causal, policy, price, refund, benefits, licensed, safety, legal, compliance, or disclosure claims.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: escalation and adjacent owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Route complaints, repeated failures, missing authority, source conflicts, family-facing claims, licensed preparation questions, benefits questions, cemetery or crematory authority, and policy exceptions to the correct owner with evidence lineage preserved.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: first-call record, removal record, custody status, case number, decedent identifiers, arranger record, service event record, and disposition method
General Price List, Casket Price List, Outer Burial Container Price List, itemized selections, cash-advance items, and Statement of Funeral Goods and Services Selected
death certificate demographic worksheet, EDRS status, medical certification status, registrar status, certified copy order, disposition permit, burial-transit permit, and amendment record
embalming authorization or declination posture, viewing request, refrigeration or delay indicators, and licensed funeral director or embalmer owner
cremation, hydrolysis, burial, cemetery, committal, interment-right, lot or niche, opening or closing, certificate of cremation, and release-of-cremated-remains records
preneed contract, trust or insurance reference, assignment record, veteran burial-support artifact, DD214 reference, itemized receipt, obituary, aftercare, and survivor communication records
complaint cases, service recovery notes, policy exception requests, customer or family communication logs, and escalation packages
metric dictionary, retailer or operator calendar, source-precedence rules, refresh timestamps, scorecards, approval matrix, and reporting templates
systems_of_record: electronic death registration system or vital-records portal
CRM, complaint, or family-service case system
identity, access, and audit logging system

allowed: Classify funeral services analytics requests and verify minimum prerequisite records, tenant facts, definitions, and source hierarchy.
Produce evidence-linked metric definitions, analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, caveat packages, resolution notes, and stakeholder communication summaries.
Define source hierarchy, denominator rules, refresh assumptions, confidence posture, and action thresholds for supported funeral service analytics.
Escalate to adjacent owners when policy exceptions, complaints, missing authority, licensed decisions, or conflicting records determine the safe answer.
Refuse unsupported trend, benchmark, causal, forecast, board-ready, external, or regulatory claims when records or methodology do not support them.
escalate: Authorizing-agent, jurisdiction, disposition path, price-disclosure, vital-record, crematory, cemetery, custody, or release evidence is missing and would change interpretation.
Funeral home case management, EDRS, document repository, crematory, cemetery, complaint, accounting, or warehouse records disagree on metric outcome.
Customer complaint, family sensitivity, price or policy exception, licensed preparation issue, benefits question, safety concern, or privacy constraint changes whether the output is safe.
Public baseline, state rule, cemetery or crematory policy, preneed term, tenant policy, or approval chain conflicts on required documents, source precedence, or metric definition.
refuse: Any legal right-of-disposition, licensed funeral directing, embalming, crematory, cemetery, vital-record, benefits, billing approval, safety disposition, privacy, compliance signoff, policy exception, or external disclosure authority request.
Requests to publish certainty when source quality, source freshness, system agreement, or metric definitions remain unresolved.
Requests to treat generic service analytics as sufficient without funeral, cemetery, crematory, vital-record, preneed, aftercare, complaint, or family-service artifacts.

domain_failure_modes: Counting arrangement completion from family approval alone while ignoring GPL, itemized selections, disposition readiness, and authorization evidence.
Reporting death-registration readiness from funeral home case status while EDRS medical certification or disposition permit status remains pending.
Mixing at-need and preneed cases, direct cremation, immediate burial, full-service burial, veterans committal, and cemetery-only services without explicit denominator rules.
Treating cremation authorization, certificate of cremation, release of cremated remains, and final disposition as interchangeable completion evidence.
Using complaint closure dates to imply family satisfaction when unresolved custody, identification, release, or policy-exception issues remain.
Comparing facility cycle times without jurisdiction, medical examiner or coroner, cemetery, crematory, staffing, holiday, or permit constraints.
Turning descriptive direct-cremation or case-mix trends into causal claims about family preference without supporting evidence.
Recommending fee waivers, refunds, package changes, or policy exceptions directly from analytics without approval authority.
Treating veterans burial allowance, SSA, insurance, preneed trust, or cemetery interment-right records as proof of eligibility or approval.
Publishing board-ready or customer-facing claims without metric methodology, source refresh, caveats, and approval context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane is governed by funeral home case-management records, Funeral Rule disclosure artifacts, EDRS and vital-record records, crematory tracking, cemetery management, preneed administration, CRM and complaint systems, aftercare systems, accounting records, reporting warehouses, and policy repositories. These records determine what can be counted, caveated, reconciled, or escalated.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: Funeral-specific failure modes include right-of-disposition ambiguity, missing GPL or itemized statement evidence, death-registration and permit conflicts, cremation authorization or custody-chain gaps, cemetery interment-right uncertainty, release-of-cremated-remains delays, family sensitivity, preneed and veterans evidence confusion, and licensed preparation or safety boundaries. Generic service analytics would miss these dependencies.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane stops at evidence-backed analytics, resolution notes, caveats, reconciliation, and escalation packaging. Legal, licensed funeral directing or embalming, crematory, cemetery, vital-record registrar, benefits, billing approval, privacy, safety, compliance signoff, and policy exception decisions belong to adjacent specialists or human approvers.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: State funeral, cemetery, cremation, hydrolysis, vital-record, and disposition rules vary, and individual cemeteries or crematories may impose additional evidence or scheduling requirements.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant jurisdiction, facility, cemetery, crematory, and source-precedence context before final analysis or publication.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant metric definitions and publication thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Tenants may define arrangement completion, service completion, case mix, aftercare completion, complaint closure, and readiness thresholds differently.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Record tenant adaptation explicitly, preserve public or prior baseline definitions, caveat comparability, and require approval before publication.
